整套首页
unit_02 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_02

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 261 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Sentence Equivalence
In Japanese aesthetics, especially but not only in Noh, beauty contains the idea of ______: beauty must have an air of evanescence, the intimation of its own demise.In Japanese aesthetics, especially but not only in Noh, beauty contains the idea of ______: beauty must have an air of evanescence, the intimation of its own demise.
答案：C, F　decay; deterioration
aestheticaesthetics
aesthetic: standard unit headword | aesthetics: standard unit headword
#2 Text Completion
For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　sacrifice; relinquishing; humility
proportionrelinquish
proportion: standard unit headword | relinquish: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
The more(i)__________ the future goal advocated by an organization’s leader, the more(ii)____ ——— it becomes in relation to the status quo, and therefore the greater will be the tendency of followers to perceive the leader as(iii)__________. conversely, leaders who set goals that are credible extensions of current realities can seen unimaginative.The more(i)__________ the future goal advocated by an organization’s leader, the more(ii)____ ——— it becomes in relation to the status quo, and therefore the greater will be the tendency of followers to perceive the leader as(iii)__________. conversely, leaders who set goals that are credible extensions of current realities can seen unimaginative.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　idealized; discrepant; visionary
converselycredible
conversely: standard unit headword | credible: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：A, D　conflict; coherence
underlieembody

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　obviates; perpetuate
undervaluedelineate
undervalue: standard unit headword | delineate: standard unit headword
#6 Sentence Equivalence
The current ______ of repackaged music under Miles Davis’ name might prompt any reasonable person to conclude that the recording vault has been plundered bare.The current ______ of repackaged music under Miles Davis’ name might prompt any reasonable person to conclude that the recording vault has been plundered bare.
答案：A, D　glut; surfeit
glutprompt
glut: standard unit headword | prompt: standard unit headword
#7 Sentence Equivalence
The Great Lakes wolf is a ______, stumping scientists as to whether it is a subspecies of the gray wolf or a distinct species.The Great Lakes wolf is a ______, stumping scientists as to whether it is a subspecies of the gray wolf or a distinct species.
答案：B, F　riddle; conundrum
prototypeparagon
prototype: standard unit headword | paragon: standard unit headword
#8 Text Completion
Anthropologist Jane Goodall was ______ in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors’ attempts to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.Anthropologist Jane Goodall was ______ in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors’ attempts to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.
答案：B　stalwart
ficklesolicitous
fickle: standard unit headword | solicitous: standard unit headword
#9 Text Completion
The material covered in this article has been (i)______ in previous publications, and since currently neglected areas remained unexplored, the article contains no (ii)______.The material covered in this article has been (i)______ in previous publications, and since currently neglected areas remained unexplored, the article contains no (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　scrutinized; revelations
counterscrutinize
counter: standard unit headword | scrutinize: standard unit headword
#10 Sentence Equivalence
The cat known to researchers as M-120—beefy, audacious, and apparently smart enough to spot a free lunch—is perhaps the world's least ______ lynx: the scientists catch him several times a year.The cat known to researchers as M-120—beefy, audacious, and apparently smart enough to spot a free lunch—is perhaps the world's least ______ lynx: the scientists catch him several times a year.
答案：B, E　evasive; elusive
furtiveaudacious
furtive: standard unit headword | audacious: standard unit headword
#11 Text Completion
The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)______ is the cowbirds’ habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)______ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)______, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)______ is the cowbirds’ habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)______ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)______, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amusing; unwitting; grow quickly
gallgalling
gall: standard unit headword | galling: standard unit headword
#12 Sentence Equivalence
Meteorology is one of the few fields of applied science that demands prediction; since prediction involves considerable uncertainty and uncertainty is ______ scientists, meteorology will continue to exist in a fraught intellectual space.Meteorology is one of the few fields of applied science that demands prediction; since prediction involves considerable uncertainty and uncertainty is ______ scientists, meteorology will continue to exist in a fraught intellectual space.
答案：B, E　anathema to; repellent to
anathemadiscredit
anathema: standard unit headword | discredit: standard unit headword
#13 Text Completion
Geologists suggest that as the most productive oil reservoirs begin to dry up, the expensive cost and high risk of drilling in the marginal area becomes less ______ and more acceptable.Geologists suggest that as the most productive oil reservoirs begin to dry up, the expensive cost and high risk of drilling in the marginal area becomes less ______ and more acceptable.
答案：A　onerous
efficaciousreservoir
efficacious: standard unit headword | reservoir: standard unit headword

答案：B　precocity

Although oceanic water currents can appear to be almost completely scientists recognize the existence of invisible separators, which they call transport barriers and these maintain a measure of order and structure in the chaotic flow.Although oceanic water currents can appear to be almost completely scientists recognize the existence of invisible separators, which they call transport barriers and these maintain a measure of order and structure in the chaotic flow.
答案：A, F　uncontrolled; random
chaotic

#17 TC SINGLE
The efficacy of a placebo may not ___________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.The efficacy of a placebo may not ___________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.
答案：C　require
efficacy
efficacy: standard unit headword
#18 TC DOUBLE
Scientists were jubilant to discover a remarkably well-preserved sixteenth-century vessel in the icy waters of the Baltic Sea, but they were (i) ___________ by the discovery, a lack of oxygen in icy depths can (ii) __________ the usual riot of creatures that like to feast on lost wooden ships.Scientists were jubilant to discover a remarkably well-preserved sixteenth-century vessel in the icy waters of the Baltic Sea, but they were (i) ___________ by the discovery, a lack of oxygen in icy depths can (ii) __________ the usual riot of creatures that like to feast on lost wooden ships.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　not entirely surprised; discourage
jubilant
jubilant: standard unit headword
#19 TC TRIPLE
The study found that every child has his or her own highly (i) ____________ daily level of activity. It is not affected by school physical education, or whether the child walks or drives to school, or how much time the child spends in front of a television. We don't know what determines this (ii) ____________ activity level. But engineering the environment to require more activity (iii) ____________ children whose nature is to be inactive.The study found that every child has his or her own highly (i) ____________ daily level of activity. It is not affected by school physical education, or whether the child walks or drives to school, or how much time the child spends in front of a television. We don't know what determines this (ii) ____________ activity level. But engineering the environment to require more activity (iii) ____________ children whose nature is to be inactive.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　consistent; intrinsic; will have little impact on
intrinsic
intrinsic: standard unit headword
#20 SE
It may be fairly easy to criticize Tatsumi's comic book on a drawing-by-drawing or line-by-line basis, but one ought not make that mistake, for the book's pleasures are __________ .It may be fairly easy to criticize Tatsumi's comic book on a drawing-by-drawing or line-by-line basis, but one ought not make that mistake, for the book's pleasures are __________ .
答案：C, F　cumulative; additive
trivial
trivial: standard unit headword
#21 TC DOUBLE
One example of a (i) __________ occurs in a vacuum state in quantum field theory. There, “something” and “nothing” are (ii) __________: it is entirely consistent for there to be nothing, and nonetheless for stuff to show up when we try to detect it.One example of a (i) __________ occurs in a vacuum state in quantum field theory. There, “something” and “nothing” are (ii) __________: it is entirely consistent for there to be nothing, and nonetheless for stuff to show up when we try to detect it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　paradox; not mutually exclusive
nonetheless
nonetheless: standard unit headword
#22 TC SINGLE
Natural selection has favored some instinctive reactions of organisms over others, so only those that promote survival and reproduction have become established, but instinctive responses need not be ____________: they may adjust over time.Natural selection has favored some instinctive reactions of organisms over others, so only those that promote survival and reproduction have become established, but instinctive responses need not be ____________: they may adjust over time.
答案：B　immutable
immutable
immutable: standard unit headword
#23 TC TRIPLE
The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) ____________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) ____________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) ____________ as time passes.The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) ____________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) ____________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) ____________ as time passes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　dynamic; sound; misleading
hazard
hazard: standard unit headword
#24 SE
The field of urban ecology is in ____________ phase: just a decade or two ago the idea of doing natural science in a city was something few researchers ever considered.The field of urban ecology is in ____________ phase: just a decade or two ago the idea of doing natural science in a city was something few researchers ever considered.
答案：B, C　an incipient; a nascent
moribund
moribund: standard unit headword
#25 TC SINGLE
__________ in the ordinary sense-meticulous attention to whether one is doing the right thing-is a trait often praised by ethical theorists, who generally think that the more time spent on self-scrutiny, the better.__________ in the ordinary sense-meticulous attention to whether one is doing the right thing-is a trait often praised by ethical theorists, who generally think that the more time spent on self-scrutiny, the better.
答案：E　Conscientiousness
trait
trait: standard unit headword
#26 Sentence Equivalence
The union states its position polemically; its leaders say they are fighting to save good jobs while ______ corporations replace full-time workers with part-time ones in order to cut down on both wages and benefits.The union states its position polemically; its leaders say they are fighting to save good jobs while ______ corporations replace full-time workers with part-time ones in order to cut down on both wages and benefits.
答案：C, F　rapacious; avaricious
rapacious
rapacious: standard unit headword
#27 TC TRIPLE
Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious
promulgate
promulgate: standard unit headword

Though Marie Cool and Fabio Balducci consider themselves visual artists rather than performance artists, their work seems________, not firmly situated in either world.Though Marie Cool and Fabio Balducci consider themselves visual artists rather than performance artists, their work seems________, not firmly situated in either world.
答案：B, E　liminal; intermediate
prototypical
prototypical: standard unit headword

Films that critics have slumbered through rarely generate industry excitement, even though the critics’ ______ reception may be less the fault of the movie than of its unfortunate time slot near a fatiguing film festival’s conclusion.Films that critics have slumbered through rarely generate industry excitement, even though the critics’ ______ reception may be less the fault of the movie than of its unfortunate time slot near a fatiguing film festival’s conclusion.
答案：A, C　somnolent; lethargic

But the cartoonist makes their lives (ii)______ in ways that do not seem (iii)______ at all. Real things happen here – births, deaths, adoptions, affairs, breakups, commitments, ceremonies, civil union – and they matter.The characters in this comic strip fret about the (i)______ of their “little counterculture lives”, especially when terrible things are happening in the world. But the cartoonist makes their lives (ii)______ in ways that do not seem (iii)______ at all. Real things happen here – births, deaths, adoptions, affairs, breakups, commitments, ceremonies, civil union – and they matter.

Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 2
Blank 3

#46 Text Completion

The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______: the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill
standstill

Friedlander reminds us that everyday objects (ii) __________ or, more exactly, that the unimpeded visual scene of a building without cars or power lines or street signs is a highly (iii) __________ photographic artifact.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　impersonal; normally interfere; contrived

答案：B　severe
fury

答案：A, E　controversial; provocative
premise

#52 Text Completion
Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)______ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)______, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)______ of critical thought.Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)______ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)______, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)______ of critical thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　an impediment to; hasty; stagnation
hasty
hasty: standard unit headword

If aging is merely an avoidable by-product of life rather than a necessary progression, it is possible that we might eventually forestall ______.If aging is merely an avoidable by-product of life rather than a necessary progression, it is possible that we might eventually forestall ______.
答案：A, C　senescence; decrepitude
progression
progression: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　inevitable; condemns
credit
credit: standard unit headword

The brain has become, for many people, ______ the biological machinations of the self, and the self-knowledge promised by neuroscience has ignited a hunger to understand how new findings weigh in on age-old questions.The brain has become, for many people, ______ the biological machinations of the self, and the self-knowledge promised by neuroscience has ignited a hunger to understand how new findings weigh in on age-old questions.
答案：A, B　tantamount to; synonymous with
machination
machination: standard unit headword
#56 Text Completion
For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　firmly anchored in; absurd; profound
anchor
anchor: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　wayward; errant
prune

Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)______ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)______ the legend.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk

Blank 2
Blank 3

If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

#104 TC TRIPLE
Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　invariably face opposition; accustomed; militate against
embody
embody: standard unit headword
#105 TC DOUBLE
Considering that experimental treatments that are (i) __________ one mouse population are, nonetheless, ineffectual with other mice, we might reasonably conclude that many rodent studies are not (ii) ___________.Considering that experimental treatments that are (i) __________ one mouse population are, nonetheless, ineffectual with other mice, we might reasonably conclude that many rodent studies are not (ii) ___________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　successful for; methodologically rigorous
nonetheless
nonetheless: standard unit headword
#106 Text Completion
For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______ : the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______ , a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to use forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______ : the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______ , a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to use forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill
standstill
standstill: standard unit headword
#107 SE
The aesthetic appeal of Sydney’s Hyde Park Obelisk was often ________ its practicality: the structure was not just an impressive display of cosmopolitan splendor but a means to vent gases from the city’s underground sewer system.The aesthetic appeal of Sydney’s Hyde Park Obelisk was often ________ its practicality: the structure was not just an impressive display of cosmopolitan splendor but a means to vent gases from the city’s underground sewer system.
答案：B, F　a distraction from; a disguise for
aesthetic
aesthetic: standard unit headword
#108 TC TRIPLE
While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　merely fortuitous; adept at; spurious
scrutinize
scrutinize: standard unit headword
#109 Text Completion
Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)______ . The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)______ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)______ , Critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)______ . The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)______ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)______ , Critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　amenable to refutation; upended; persistent

答案：B　interrogated

This apparent (i)______ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii)______ , noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.Rocks of the Morrison Formation in the western United States yield many dinosaur fossils but few plant fossils. This apparent (i)______ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii)______ , noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.

Blank 2

For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seem merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______ . To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seem merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______ . In the midst of initially (ii)______ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)______ .Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)______ . As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)______ .

Blank 3

Just because, as a photographer, Friedlander (i)______ places that most people consider ugly does not mean that he is out to prove they are beautiful. Instead, his work suggests that the photographer simply cannot ignore so much of the built American landscape but is obligated to (ii)______ what we pass through day in and day out, regardless of (iii)______.Just because, as a photographer, Friedlander (i)______ places that most people consider ugly does not mean that he is out to prove they are beautiful. Instead, his work suggests that the photographer simply cannot ignore so much of the built American landscape but is obligated to (ii)______ what we pass through day in and day out, regardless of (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　is interested in; document; aesthetics

答案：C, D　a decisive; an unmistakable

nonetheless: standard unit headword

Laughter, like speech, is a primarily human faculty, although both of these functions may also exist in a more __________ form in other primates.Laughter, like speech, is a primarily human faculty, although both of these functions may also exist in a more __________ form in other primates.
答案：A, B　rudimentary; elementary

underlie: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　distinct; idiosyncratic

aesthetic: standard unit headword

答案：A, D　judicious; sensible

solicitous: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　unwarranted; groundless

#165 SE

答案：D, F　coercive; imperious

答案：A, C　abused; misused

underlie: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　prescribe; codify

答案：E, F　defied; disregarded

答案：A, F　hamper; impede

答案：D, F　placate; appease

答案：D, F　spurious; specious

答案：A, B　harmless; innocuous

答案：C, F　precise; circumspect

答案：E, F　tempered; modulated

答案：C, F　prevalent; widespread

O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. #216 Text Completion
Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive
conversely
conversely: standard unit headword
#217 Text Completion
Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______ , that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______ , but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______ .Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______ , that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______ , but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious

intrinsic: standard unit headword

答案：E　follow

答案：B, E, H　scientific; admitted; necessary characteristic
